text: I feel completely hopeless. Nothing I try makes any difference.
I dread waking up every morning. The day feels like a burden before it starts.
I've been crying without knowing why. It's been going on for weeks.
I can't concentrate on anything. My mind keeps going to dark places.
I feel numb most of the time. Like my emotions are turned off.
Work is unbearable. I feel like I'm suffocating there.
My relationship is falling apart and I don't know how to fix it.
I feel worthless. Like I don't contribute anything meaningful.
I can't sleep properly. I lie awake replaying all my mistakes.
I've stopped enjoying things I used to love. Everything feels grey.
My anxiety is at an all-time high. Even small tasks feel enormous.
I feel like I'm failing my family every single day.
I've been isolating myself. I don't have the energy to interact with people.
The loneliness is crushing. I feel invisible to everyone around me.
I lost my job and I feel completely lost. I don't know who I am without it.
My depression medication doesn't seem to be working. I feel worse.
I've been having panic attacks twice a week. I'm scared to go outside.
My marriage is loveless. We live like roommates. I'm deeply unhappy.
I feel trapped in a life I didn't choose. There's no joy in anything.
I've been crying since I found out about the miscarriage. I can't stop.
I'm exhausted from pretending to be okay at work.
I hate looking in the mirror. I feel disgusting.
My intrusive thoughts are getting worse. I'm scared of my own mind.
I've been having suicidal thoughts. Not a plan, but thoughts.
I feel like a burden to my family. They'd be better off without me.
I screamed into a pillow today. I don't know how much more I can take.
My grief comes in waves. Today is a bad day.
I feel angry all the time for no obvious reason. I snap at everyone.
I've been having nightmares about the accident. I wake up terrified.
I don't feel like myself anymore. I can't remember the last time I felt okay.
I'm fine, don't worry about me.
Everything is great, honestly. Never been better.
I've been keeping myself very busy lately.
I'm managing. It's all under control.
No complaints. Life goes on.
I shouldn't complain. Others have it worse.
I'm dealing with it. I always do.
Things could be worse, I suppose.
I've been staying productive. That helps.
I don't really talk about these things. I prefer to handle things myself.
I feel okay. Not great, not bad.
It's been a mixed week. Some good moments, some difficult ones.
I'm not sure how I feel right now. Kind of in between.
Things are what they are. I'm taking it day by day.
I've had better weeks but I've also had worse.
My mood has been stable. Nothing dramatic either way.
I completed the exercises. I haven't noticed a big difference yet.
I'm attending the sessions because my company recommended it.
I'm not sure if therapy is working yet. It's only been a few sessions.
I feel fairly neutral today. I wanted to check in.
I've been okay. No major crises, but no real progress either.
I understand what you're saying but I'm not sure how to apply it.
My energy levels are moderate. I've been eating and sleeping normally.
I'm cautiously optimistic. Things seem slightly better.
I don't feel worse than before, but I'm not sure I feel better either.
I feel a lot better this week. The journaling is making a difference.
I finally talked to my manager and it went surprisingly well.
I had a really good therapy session. I feel lighter.
I completed 30 days of meditation. I feel genuinely more grounded.
Things at home are improving. My partner and I communicated better this week.
I got the promotion. It's a relief after all the hard work.
I spoke to my parents about my feelings. They listened. It was healing.
My anxiety has reduced significantly since starting medication.
I feel proud of myself for asking for help. That was hard.
I slept 8 hours last night. It's been months since that happened.
I'm starting to feel like myself again. Slowly, but surely.
I signed up for the exercise class I've been putting off for a year.
I feel hopeful for the first time in a long time.
I set a boundary at work and my manager actually respected it.
My relationship is stronger after the couples counselling.
I passed my exam. The hard work paid off.
I'm not fully healed but I can see the path forward now.
I'm grateful for this platform. It has genuinely helped me.
I took a real holiday for the first time in 3 years. No emails. I feel restored.
I opened up to a colleague today. It felt good to not be alone in this.
